summary_one_line: Input must encode the IPv6 next-header chain so the extension-header dispatch reaches the exact kProtoDstOpts switch arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A next-header byte (chained through the extension-header walk) that equals the exact protocol constant kProtoDstOpts, so the switch in HandleExtensionHeaders selects `case kProtoDstOpts:` rather than kProtoHopOpts / kProtoFragment / default.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds resolve the branch 10/10 with hitcount 1239 and reach kProtoDstOpts (W hits the case 900 arm and HandleFragment/HandleOptions), the byte fields driving the dispatch being precisely placed concentrated values (e.g. header bytes 78/77 and 66/67 at offsets 0x00-0x01).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds block 8/10 with hitcount only 70; their next-header bytes land them on kProtoHopOpts / default arms (L hits case 890 and default 911 but never the case-900 true side), the L bytes being scattered 0x44/0x30 fill rather than the exact dispatch constant.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer CMP that compares aNextHeader against the kProtoDstOpts switch constant, records both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the required protocol constant into the next-header byte; naive must hit the exact constant by blind havoc, which it does only 2/10 times.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the kProtoDstOpts switch arm inside HandleExtensionHeaders.
The branch is a switch dispatch on aNextHeader selecting among protocol constants.
Winner reaches the kProtoDstOpts arm while loser does not; W takes case 900, L stays on the false side.
cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 with high hitcount while naive blocks 8/10 with low hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1239  loser=70

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolves only 2/10 by blind mutation, the loser role of the I2S pair.
Winner and loser differ at concentrated header byte offsets driving the dispatch.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog intercepts integer CMPs and records both operands keyed by PC.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Each CMP callback records
both operands into a per-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer keyed by
PC.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so it must hit the dispatch constant by blind havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13321 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 04f7c870a8101848` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 32 of the 33-level ancestor chain; under the current build this nameless ParentInfo-only ancestor is lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in the cmplog lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch arm constant is randomized per-build (defeating CMP operand logging) showing cmplog resolving the kProtoDstOpts arm no faster than naive.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets 0x00-0x01 are header fields whose exact mapping to aNextHeader through the chained extension-header walk is inferred from source structure, not directly traced to the intercepted CMP.
